rationale: The citing paper uses MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) as specific prior evidence that ad creative content affects outcomes, noting its finding that emotional TV content is more sales-effective than rational appeal. It then positions its own contribution as extending prior econometric work on advertising content and dynamics, explicitly including this paper, into digital display advertising. This role is part of the conceptual framing and research motivation, not just a generic bibliography mention.
relation_description: Bruce, Murthi, and Rao (2017) extend MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) from offline TV ad-content effects to a digital-ad setting, testing how message content interacts with format, targeting, and carry-over over time. The cited paper provides a key empirical precedent for why content effects should be modeled rather than treated as homogeneous.
